title: Dataset Pipeline
text:
Repo: zzsi/openscore on HuggingFace


The OpenScore corpus provides public-domain MusicXML scores from IMSLP:



Corpus
Scores
Training pages




Lieder
1,460

Quartets

6,274


Orchestra

4,870


Total
1,676
14,456






MusicXML (from OpenScore)
  → LilyPond Docker render → SVG pages
  → SVG bar number extraction → per-page measure ranges
  → music21 slice → per-page MusicXML
  → PNG conversion → (image, MusicXML) pairs
  → HuggingFace push
Implementation: datasets/omr/openscore/page_musicxml.py




Images: 835x1181 pixels (LilyPond default DPI)
Bar numbers: Extracted from SVG <text> elements with font-size heuristic
Pickup bars: Auto-detected (measure[0].number == 0) with offset adjustment
Page breaks: Measures spanning page boundaries assigned to next page





3/500 lieder samples have malformed XML (music21 export bugs)
7/27,632 lyrics contain non-breaking spaces (\xa0)
See datasets/omr/openscore/page_musicxml_runs.md for full run log

title: Dataset Pipeline
text:
OLiMPiC Dataset
Repo: zzsi/olimpic on HuggingFace
System-level piano crops from the OLiMPiC dataset (ICDAR 2024):

15,014 training samples (synthetic variant)
System-level crops (~3000x700 pixels) — one grand staff per image
Piano only — no voice staff, but partial lyrics may be visible at top of crop
Ground truth: MusicXML + Linearized MusicXML (LMX)

Implementation: datasets/omr/olimpic/prepare.py

title: Evaluation
text:
We use alignment-aware sequence matching (SequenceMatcher) to compare predicted and reference MXC note sequences. This handles insertions and deletions without cascading positional mismatches.


The primary metric. Compares only pitched notes (excludes rests) between prediction and reference using sequence alignment.
A sample with many rest measures followed by a few notes would score 0% on rests-included metrics even if the notes are correct. Pitched-only avoids this.










Metric
What it measures




Pitched-only similarity
Correct notes in correct order (excl. rests)


Positional accuracy
% of notes correct at each position (strict)


Rhythm similarity
Note types/durations correct


Combined similarity
Pitch + type together


Note coverage
Predicted events / reference events


Longest match
Longest consecutive correct run


Header accuracy
Key, time signature, part structure







< 10%: Model not reading pitch from image
10-30%: Some pitch learning, with alignment drift
30-50%: Meaningful pitch reading, room to improve
> 50%: Good accuracy (check longest match for consistency)
Coverage > 100%: Model hallucinating extra notes

title: Evaluation
text:
We use alignment-aware sequence matching (SequenceMatcher) to compare predicted and reference MXC note sequences. This handles insertions and deletions without cascading positional mismatches.


The primary metric. Compares only pitched notes (excludes rests) between prediction and reference using sequence alignment.
A sample with many rest measures followed by a few notes would score 0% on rests-included metrics even if the notes are correct. Pitched-only avoids this.










Metric
What it measures




Pitched-only similarity
Correct notes in correct order (excl. rests)


Positional accuracy
% of notes correct at each position (strict)


Rhythm similarity
Note types/durations correct


Combined similarity
Pitch + type together


Note coverage
Predicted events / reference events


Longest match
Longest consecutive correct run


Header accuracy
Key, time signature, part structure







< 10%: Model not reading pitch from image
10-30%: Some pitch learning, with alignment drift
30-50%: Meaningful pitch reading, room to improve
> 50%: Good accuracy (check longest match for consistency)
Coverage > 100%: Model hallucinating extra notes

title: Evaluation
text:
Usage
# Evaluate latest WandB run
python eval_mxc.py --latest

# Specific run with trend across steps
python eval_mxc.py --wandb-run wandb/run-XXXXX --by-step

# Compare two MXC files directly
python eval_mxc.py --pred prediction.mxc --ref reference.mxc

title: Evaluation
text:
Caveats

Metrics are computed on 10 held-out samples during training, not the full dev set (193 samples). Results may not be statistically robust.
SequenceMatcher finds the longest common subsequence, which can match notes that happen to share a pitch but are in different measures.
The metric does not distinguish between a wrong pitch and a missing note followed by a correct one — both reduce the similarity score.
Lyric order (L1 vs L2) sometimes differs between prediction and reference without affecting musical content.

title: Evaluation
text:
Implementation

Script: vlm_omr_sft/eval_mxc.py
Guide: vlm_omr_sft/reports/evaluation-guide.md

title: MXC Format
text: MusicXML is extremely verbose. A typical lieder page produces ~14K tokens — far beyond the 4,096-token VLM context window. Only ~10% of pages fit, and models spend all inference tokens on XML preamble before reaching any <note> elements.

title: MXC Format
text: MusicXML is extremely verbose. A typical lieder page produces ~14K tokens — far beyond the 4,096-token VLM context window. Only ~10% of pages fit, and models spend all inference tokens on XML preamble before reaching any <note> elements.

title: MXC Format
text:
MXC Design
MXC is a line-based encoding where one note = one line:
header work-title="Just for Today"
header composer="Jane Bingham Abbott"
P1 Voice Ob
P2 Piano Pno
---
P1
M 1 div=10080 key=-1 time=3/4 clef=G2
dir @above [font-weight=bold] Andante
N C4 q 10080 su L1:s:Lord,
N Bb3 e 5040 su bm=begin L1:s:for
N C4 e 5040 su bm=end L1:b:to

title: MXC Format
text:
Compression
Tested on 500 lieder pages:



Metric
Cleaned XML
MXC




Median chars
50,272
4,172


Median ~tokens
~14,363
~1,192


Compression
—
12x

title: MXC Format
text:
Round-trip accuracy

99.2% lossless round-trip on 500 samples
3 failures from malformed XML in source data
1 failure from double-space in lyric text

title: MXC Format
text:
What MXC preserves

Pitches, durations, types, accidentals, dots
Stems, beams, ties, slurs
Lyrics (bilingual, syllabic encoding)
Key/time signatures, clefs
Dynamics, tempo text, directions
Composer, lyricist, title
Barlines, repeats, endings

title: MXC Format
text:
Implementation

Encoder/decoder: vlm_omr_sft/mxc.py
Format spec: vlm_omr_sft/MXC.md
Unit tests: vlm_omr_sft/test_mxc.py (28 tests)
Real-data tests: vlm_omr_sft/test_mxc_realdata.py (500 samples)

title: CVlization
text: 190+ state-of-the-art open source AI capabilities, packaged in self-contained Docker environments. Find a recipe, test it, copy what you need.

title: CVlization
text:
Featured Projects

Optical Music Recognition
Fine-tuning Vision-Language Models to transcribe sheet music images into symbolic notation. We developed MXC (MusicXML Compact) to compress training targets 12x, then tested 6 VLMs across multiple LoRA ranks and training configurations.
Current best: 35% pitched-only similarity using Qwen3.5-9B r=32 on 3K lieder pages. The model reads pitch from staff positions but accuracy is limited by training data size.

title: CVlization
text:
Repository

Code: github.com/kungfuai/CVlization
Datasets: huggingface.co/zzsi

title: Experiments
text: We ran SFT experiments on 6 VLMs with two target formats (XML and MXC), multiple LoRA ranks, and up to 6 epochs. All training on 3K lieder pages from OpenScore with a single NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 (95 GB VRAM).

title: Experiments
text: We ran SFT experiments on 6 VLMs with two target formats (XML and MXC), multiple LoRA ranks, and up to 6 epochs. All training on 3K lieder pages from OpenScore with a single NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 (95 GB VRAM).

title: Experiments
text:
XML target format (all failed)
With raw MusicXML targets, no model generated actual pitched notes. The XML preamble consumed the entire inference token budget.



Model
train_loss
Notes




Gemma-3 4B
—
Learned XML syntax, wrong key/time/tempo


Ministral-3 r=32
0.120
Degenerate ID repetition


Ministral-3 r=8
0.173
Stuck on MIDI metadata


Qwen3.5-9B
0.187
93 notes, all rests


DeepSeek-OCR-2
0.312
29 notes, all rests



Data cleaning (stripping non-visible metadata, comments, MIDI blocks) reduced eval_loss from 0.158 to 0.048 on Ministral-3 but still produced no pitched notes.

title: Experiments

title: Experiments
text:
What works and what doesn’t
Works well:

MXC format syntax (never produces invalid MXC)
Part structure, key signatures (80%), lyrics
Pitch on simpler passages (up to 88% positional accuracy)
123 consecutive matching events on best run

Struggles with:

Dense piano polyphony
Very long pages (coverage drops with 400+ notes)
Pages where voice enters late (rests consume token budget)

title: Experiments
text:
Detailed reports

2026-03-14: Gemma-3 initial run
2026-03-18: Model comparison & data cleaning
2026-03-19: MXC experiments

title: Optical Music Recognition with VLMs
text: We fine-tune Vision-Language Models (VLMs) to transcribe sheet music page images into symbolic notation. The key innovation is MXC (MusicXML Compact), a line-based encoding that achieves 12x compression over MusicXML, enabling VLMs to generate actual musical notes within their context window.

title: Optical Music Recognition with VLMs
text: We fine-tune Vision-Language Models (VLMs) to transcribe sheet music page images into symbolic notation. The key innovation is MXC (MusicXML Compact), a line-based encoding that achieves 12x compression over MusicXML, enabling VLMs to generate actual musical notes within their context window.

title: Optical Music Recognition with VLMs
text:
Key Findings

MXC format is essential — without it, no model reaches note content
Architecture > size — Qwen3.5-9B (9.5B) beats Qwen3-VL-32B (32B)
Models < 8B fail — cannot learn pitch regardless of architecture
LoRA r=32 is optimal — tested r=8, 16, 32, 64
Data is the bottleneck — model converged at 35% on 3K samples
